title: Fuel Cell Engineers
description: Design, evaluate, modify, or construct fuel cell components or systems for transportation, stationary, or portable applications.

tasks.text: Plan or conduct experiments to validate new materials, optimize startup protocols, reduce conditioning time, or examine contaminant tolerance.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide technical consultation or direction related to the development or production of fuel cell systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Characterize component or fuel cell performances by generating operating maps, defining operating conditions, identifying design refinements, or executing durability assessments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Plan or implement fuel cell cost reduction or product improvement projects in collaboration with other engineers, suppliers, support personnel, or customers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct fuel cell testing projects, using fuel cell test stations, analytical instruments, or electrochemical diagnostics, such as cyclic voltammetry or impedance spectroscopy.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Analyze fuel cell or related test data, using statistical software.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Conduct post-service or failure analyses, using electromechanical diagnostic principles or procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Define specifications for fuel cell materials.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Recommend or implement changes to fuel cell system designs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Validate design of fuel cells, fuel cell components, or fuel cell systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Read current literature, attend meetings or conferences, or talk with colleagues to stay abreast of new technology or competitive products.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare test stations, instrumentation, or data acquisition systems for use in specific tests of fuel cell components or systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop fuel cell materials or fuel cell test equipment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Fabricate prototypes of fuel cell components, assemblies, stacks, or systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Manage fuel cell battery hybrid system architecture, including sizing of components, such as fuel cells, energy storage units, or electric drives.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Design or implement fuel cell testing or development programs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Write technical reports or proposals related to engineering projects.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Simulate or model fuel cell, motor, or other system information, using simulation software programs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Design fuel cell systems, subsystems, stacks, assemblies, or components, such as electric traction motors or power electronics.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Identify or define vehicle and system integration challenges for fuel cell vehicles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Calculate the efficiency or power output of a fuel cell system or process.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Coordinate fuel cell engineering or test schedules with departments outside engineering, such as manufacturing.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Authorize release of fuel cell parts, components, or subsystems for production.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Evaluate the power output, system cost, or environmental impact of new hydrogen or non-hydrogen fuel cell system designs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Integrate electric drive subsystems with other vehicle systems to optimize performance or mitigate faults.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop or evaluate systems or methods of hydrogen storage for fuel cell applications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
